chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with justcallmekoko/ESP32-Deauther (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with zynon/LdapRelayScan (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with zodiacon/HookHunter (score=9); pair in the chain planner.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the wifi category. Fits when the engagement needs: Scan for WiFi devices, block selected connections, create dozens of networks and confuse WiFi scanners.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Scan for WiFi devices, block selected connections, create dozens of networks and confuse WiFi scanners.
documentation.how_to_use: Clone or install `esp8266_deauther` from its upstream repository, then run `esp8266_deauther --help` (or the README entrypoint) to confirm flags. Scan for WiFi devices, block selected connections, create dozens of networks and confuse WiFi scanners.

name: esp8266_deauther
summary: Scan for WiFi devices, block selected connections, create dozens of networks and confuse WiFi scanners.

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need wifi, use esp8266_deauther because Scan for WiFi devices, block selected connections, create dozens of networks and confuse WiFi scanners.
When chaining wifi tools, pair esp8266_deauther with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `esp8266_deauther --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
